behaviors.description: When a required key is absent from both the file and the environment, the error message names the missing key by its exact config path (e.g., `database.host`).
behaviors.justification: Failure risk is in handoff across file load + env override + validation + error formatting, not a single pure function. System was considered but rejected because the behavior text does not require subprocess/network/external contract or OS-specific side-effect verification; it is in-process config composition. Agentic/Workflow are disqualified because no AI/ML decision is involved.
behaviors.test_description: Run the real loader with a temp config file missing `database.host` and `TOOL_DATABASE_HOST` unset; assert startup fails and emitted error includes exact path `database.host`.

behaviors.description: When the environment variable `XDG_CONFIG_HOME` is set, the loader resolves the config file path relative to that directory rather than `~/.config`.
behaviors.justification: This checks module wiring between env reading and path resolution. Unit-only tests can miss real precedence between `XDG_CONFIG_HOME` and default path in startup flow. System was considered but rejected: behavior text asks path-selection logic, not subprocess/HTTP/external contract validation. Agentic/Workflow are disqualified (no model output dependence).
behaviors.test_description: Set process env `XDG_CONFIG_HOME` to a temp dir, place config there, run loader startup path, and assert it reads that file instead of `~/.config/tool/config.yaml`.

behaviors.description: When an environment variable with the `TOOL_` prefix conflicts with a key in the config file, the environment variable value is used.
behaviors.justification: The bug surface is merge precedence across config-file parser and env-override mapper. Unit tests on either parser alone can give false confidence. System was considered but rejected because no explicit external contract or OS/subprocess behavior is required in the behavior text. Agentic/Workflow are disqualified because correctness does not depend on AI output.
behaviors.test_description: Create config with key `database.host=file.example`, set `TOOL_DATABASE_HOST=env.example`, run loader, and assert effective value is `env.example`.

behaviors.description: When the config file contains a YAML syntax error, the error message includes the line number where the problem was detected.
behaviors.justification: Need real interaction with YAML parser exception data and CLI error formatting; mocking parser errors can hide mismatch in line-number propagation. System was considered but rejected because behavior text does not require external protocol/OS contract checks beyond in-process parsing. Agentic/Workflow are disqualified (no AI/ML dependency).
behaviors.test_description: Write malformed YAML in a temp config file, run loader, and assert failure message includes the parser-reported line number.

behaviors.description: When the config directory does not exist at startup, the loader creates it and writes a defaults file before proceeding.
behaviors.justification: This explicitly requires proving an externally visible filesystem side effect (directory and file creation) via round-trip observation at startup, which matches SYSTEM TRIAD trigger #3 and real OS behavior. Integration was considered but rejected because behavior text requires real boundary side effects, not just in-memory orchestration. Agentic/Workflow are disqualified (no AI pipeline/model judgment).
behaviors.test_description: Start CLI/loader with `XDG_CONFIG_HOME` pointing to a non-existent temp path; verify process proceeds, then read back filesystem to confirm directory exists and defaults file was created with expected baseline content.
